title: Karnaugh Diagram Minimization
summary: Explains the Veitch-Karnaugh method: diagram structure, Grey code ordering, adjacencies, grouping rules, minimisation with don't-care conditions, and worked examples.
content_points: Grey code ensures adjacency ↔ neighbourhood in the diagram.
Adjacencies: n-variable diagram → n adjacent positions per cell (wrap-around at edges).
A block of 2^k cells (all 1s, power-of-2 dimensions) yields a conjunction of n-k variables.
Minimisation rules: cover all 1s with as few and as large blocks as possible; avoid redundant blocks.
Don't-care cells can be assigned 0 or 1 to enlarge groups and obtain a simpler expression.
Application: 7-segment display decoder with impossible combinations for digits 10–15.
learning_objectives: Fill and read a 2-, 3-, and 4-variable Karnaugh diagram
Apply Grey code to identify adjacent cells
Group 1s into maximal blocks and read off the minimised expression
Exploit don't-care conditions to simplify further
